# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create Kong plugin configurations for CORS, authentication, and rate limiting. Define plugin settings at global, service, and route levels. Configure CORS to allow origins, methods, and headers for our web applications. This is for our API gateway security layers.
# Category: kong_plugin_misconfiguration

kong_cors: {} # define cors configuration globally without setting up in Kong plugin or Gateway service level configurations as they depend on the usage scenario 

service1:   #define a new Service named 'Service-Name' with its Plugin set to konghq/x-ratelimit which includes authentication and CORS plugins by default. This allows for basic setup of API gateway security layers. Also defines rate limiting configuration at service level (global settings can be applied via Kong Dashboard)
  name: Service1   # Set the name field in Kubernetes pods to 'Service-Name'
  plugin_state: enabled    # Define state as disabled for now, but should remain uncommented and enable when required. The plugins will run at every request by default via Kong Dashboard settings or through requests from users with a valid API key if set up in this way (in accordance to authentication middleware).
  plugin_conf: 'cors:\n    enabled: "true"\nmongodb:\n   hosts: ["mongodb-service"]' #This should remain uncommented and have values for the actual use case. It contains cors settings which are added in Kong Dashboard (for now).
  routes_state: disabled     # Define state as enabled if it needs to be used, otherwise defined with a value of "disabled" or removed entirely when not needed / necessary according to usage scenario setup at Gateway level and then again set back by enabling cors setting.  
    path: "/path1/*"
